title: Nephrodium singaporianum
taxonomy: Nephrodium singaporianum belongs to the family Matoniaceae. It is a type of fern, classified under the division Pteridophyta. This plant is further classified under the class Polypodiopsida and the order Matoniales. The genus Nephrodium is often considered a synonym of the genus Matonia, but some sources still use Nephrodium as a valid genus. Therefore, the classification of Nephrodium singaporianum can be considered as a member of the genus Nephrodium under the family Matoniaceae.
uses: Nephrodium singaporianum, also known as Singaporean maidenhair, is a species of fern that has been traditionally used in medicine in various parts of the world. Its medicinal uses include treating digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and acting as an antiseptic. In traditional Asian medicine, it is believed to have properties that help in treating fever, colds, and respiratory issues. The plant is also used in some cultures to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of antioxidants and other bioactive compounds.
